mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 110 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 110 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.8 million signals a weakening supply of new housing that reduces construction activity and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 1.8M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 12.5 billion signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 12.5B are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 200 thousand signals a strengthening labor market that reduces unemployment and boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200K are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal income above 360 billion signals a strengthening supply of consumer goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 360B are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
